title: Chocolate cupcakes
subtitle: Rich cocoa sponge with chocolate buttercream
excerpt: A moist chocolate cupcake built on a cocoa sponge with vegetable oil for a tender crumb. Topped with a simple dark chocolate buttercream piped in a swirl.
sourceNotes: The American cupcake (individual cake baked in a paper case, heavily frosted) became a fixture of British home baking in the 1990s and early 2000s alongside the growth of boutique cake shops. This recipe uses a cocoa sponge adapted from the method in The Hummingbird Bakery Cookbook (Tim Malouf, 2009), the book most directly responsible for the UK cupcake revival, with adjustments to the buttercream ratio for home baking.

recipe.freezeNotes: Freeze the un-iced sponges only, in a sealed bag, for up to 2 months. Buttercream and ice after thawing.
recipe.makeAheadNotes: The sponges can be baked the day before and stored in an airtight tin. Ice on the day of serving.

baking.decoratingTechnique: chocolate buttercream piped in a swirl with a star nozzle

content.text: Oil-based chocolate sponges stay moister for longer than butter-based ones. For a cupcake that will sit for several hours before eating, this matters. The buttercream is generous by design; a stiff swirl holds its shape and looks better at a distance than a thin smear.

Fill each paper case no more than two-thirds full. The sponge rises and the top should dome just above the case rim, not spill over the edge. Overfilled cases collapse when cool and the top falls away from the sides.

Heat the oven to 170°C fan. Line a 12-hole muffin tin with paper cases. Sift the {{plain-flour}}, {{cocoa-powder}}, {{caster-sugar}}, {{baking-powder}}, {{bicarbonate-of-soda}}, and {{fine-sea-salt}} into a large bowl. In a jug, whisk together the {{eggs}}, {{whole-milk}}, {{vegetable-oil}}, and {{vanilla-extract}}. Pour the wet ingredients into the dry and whisk until smooth. Divide evenly between the cases (about 60 g each). Bake for 18 to 20 minutes until a skewer comes out clean. Cool in the tin for 5 minutes, then transfer to a rack to cool completely.

Beat the {{unsalted-butter}} until smooth and pale. Sift in the {{icing-sugar}} and {{cocoa-powder}} and beat on low speed to combine, then increase the speed and beat for 3 minutes until fluffy. Add the {{whole-milk}} a tablespoon at a time to reach a pipeable consistency. Transfer to a piping bag fitted with a star nozzle and pipe a swirl onto each cooled cupcake, starting at the outside edge and spiralling inward.
